verses.text: Moab fears Israel and dreads their multitude. Fear. The nations tremble at God's people.
Moab fears Israel and dreads their multitude. Fear. The nations tremble at God's people.
Moab fears Israel and dreads their multitude. Fear. The nations tremble at God's people.
Moab fears Israel and dreads their multitude. Fear. The nations tremble at God's people.
Balak sends elders to hire Balaam to curse Israel. Hired curse. He seeks to buy a prophet.
Balak sends elders to hire Balaam to curse Israel. Hired curse. He seeks to buy a prophet.
Balak sends elders to hire Balaam to curse Israel. Hired curse. He seeks to buy a prophet.
Balaam asks God, who says, 'Thou shalt not curse... they are blessed.' Forbidden. God will not let His own be cursed.
Balaam asks God, who says, 'Thou shalt not curse... they are blessed.' Forbidden. God will not let His own be cursed.
Balaam asks God, who says, 'Thou shalt not curse... they are blessed.' Forbidden. God will not let His own be cursed.
Balaam asks God, who says, 'Thou shalt not curse... they are blessed.' Forbidden. God will not let His own be cursed.
Balaam refuses to go; the princes return. Refused. At first he obeys.
Balaam refuses to go; the princes return. Refused. At first he obeys.
Balaam refuses to go; the princes return. Refused. At first he obeys.
Balak sends greater princes and greater reward. More gold. Temptation presses harder.
Balak sends greater princes and greater reward. More gold. Temptation presses harder.
Balak sends greater princes and greater reward. More gold. Temptation presses harder.
Balaam says he cannot go beyond God's word, yet God lets him go. Permitted. God gives him over to his desire.
Balaam says he cannot go beyond God's word, yet God lets him go. Permitted. God gives him over to his desire.
Balaam says he cannot go beyond God's word, yet God lets him go. Permitted. God gives him over to his desire.
As he rides, the angel withstands him; the ass turns aside. The angel. God blocks the greedy prophet.
As he rides, the angel withstands him; the ass turns aside. The angel. God blocks the greedy prophet.
As he rides, the angel withstands him; the ass turns aside. The angel. God blocks the greedy prophet.
The ass crushes his foot, then falls; Balaam smites her. Beaten. He sees less than his beast.
The ass crushes his foot, then falls; Balaam smites her. Beaten. He sees less than his beast.
The ass crushes his foot, then falls; Balaam smites her. Beaten. He sees less than his beast.
The ass crushes his foot, then falls; Balaam smites her. Beaten. He sees less than his beast.
The ass speaks, rebuking Balaam. The ass speaks. God opens a dumb mouth to shame him.
The ass speaks, rebuking Balaam. The ass speaks. God opens a dumb mouth to shame him.
The ass speaks, rebuking Balaam. The ass speaks. God opens a dumb mouth to shame him.
Balaam sees the angel and bows; the ass saved his life. Eyes opened. He sees his folly at last.
Balaam sees the angel and bows; the ass saved his life. Eyes opened. He sees his folly at last.
Balaam sees the angel and bows; the ass saved his life. Eyes opened. He sees his folly at last.
He offers to turn back; the angel bids him speak only God's word. Sent on. He goes, but under restraint.
He offers to turn back; the angel bids him speak only God's word. Sent on. He goes, but under restraint.
Balak meets him; Balaam can speak only what God gives. Restrained. The hireling is bound by God.
Balak meets him; Balaam can speak only what God gives. Restrained. The hireling is bound by God.
Balak meets him; Balaam can speak only what God gives. Restrained. The hireling is bound by God.
Balak takes him up to see the people. The view. He prepares to curse them.
Balak takes him up to see the people. The view. He prepares to curse them.
